basics.name: Apoorva Vashisth
basics.label: PhD Student
basics.summary: I develop learning-based methods that enable intelligent robots and multi-robot systems to reason, plan, and act in complex environments.

work.name: Multi Agent Robotic Motion Lab, National University of Singapore
highlights: Developed an adaptive, reinforcement learning based method for Informative Path Planning in an obstacle-free 2D environment subject to budget constraints.
Our work was accepted at the 6th Annual Conference on Robot Learning.

work.name: Lab for Photogrammetry and Robotics, University of Bonn
highlights: Proposed a novel dynamic graph approach for adaptive informative motion planning to map targets-of-interest in 3D environment in presence of unknown obstacles.
First author work published in IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters.
B.Tech Thesis on 'Modeling and Energy Optimization of 3-Wheeled Omnidirectional Mobile Robot (TOMR)'
M.Tech Thesis on 'Voronoi Partitioning Based Multi-agent Path Planning'
Thesis focusing on 'Temporal Context for Robust Robot Planning: Integrating History Awareness and Future Prediction'

awards.title: NTSE Scholar
awards.summary: The National Talent Search Examination (NTSE) is a prestigious, government-funded scholarship program in India aiming to identify and financially support exceptionally talented 10th-grade students pursuing higher education in basic sciences, social sciences, medicine, and engineering.

awards.title: International Internship Scholarship Award
awards.summary: The International Internship Award IITKGP is a prestigious scholarship program offered by IIT Kharagpur in association with the IITKGP Foundation of USA. It provides financial support to meritorious undergraduate and graduate students pursuing high-quality research and professional internships at top global institutions.

awards.title: Ross Fellowship
awards.summary: Ross Fellowship is a highly competitive, prestigious recruitment award offered by Purdue University to recruit outstanding Ph.D.-track students.

awards.title: Herbold Scholarship
awards.summary: The Herbold Scholarship is a financial award established by the Herbold Foundation—founded by former Microsoft COO Bob Herbold—to support outstanding graduate students in engineering, computer science, and data science.

publications.name: Zur Elektrodynamik bewegter Körper
publications.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

publications.name: Über einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt
publications.summary: In the second paper, he applied the quantum theory to light to explain the photoelectric effect. In particular, he used the idea of light quanta (photons) to explain experimental results, but stressed the importance of the experimental results. The importance of his work on the photoelectric effect earned him the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

publications.name: Die Grundlage der allgemeinen Relativitätstheorie
publications.summary: The publication of the theory of general relativity made him internationally famous. He was professor of physics at the universities of Zurich (1909–1911) and Prague (1911–1912), before he returned to ETH Zurich (1912–1914).

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
